title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-four Americas markets are operationalizing AML/CFT frameworks as U.S. federal agencies finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin supervision and fundamental BSA reform while states race to establish competing crypto licensing regimes. The U.S. accounts for 16 of 28 regional events, with FinCEN's June 9 comment deadline on BSA/AML program reform representing the most significant compliance infrastructure overhaul since the Bank Secrecy Act's inception. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-one European Union members are simultaneously implementing MiCA Level 2 technical standards while AMLA becomes operational with direct supervision authority over highest-risk crypto firms. UK regulators are setting hard authorization deadlines as the FCA consultation on final conduct rules closes in June, and Middle East jurisdictions from UAE to Saudi Arabia are finalizing licensing frameworks. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific markets are simultaneously moving from travel rule implementation to enforcement, VASP licensing deadlines to active supervision, and retail-only frameworks to institutional custody access. Australia's AUSTRAC has shifted to penalty enforcement while South Korea opens traditional financial institutions to crypto custody services.
